testcase.name: test_200

error.message: failed on setup with "pytest.PytestRemovedIn9Warning: 'test_200' requested an async fixture 'client', with no plugin or hook that handled it. This is usually an error, as pytest does not natively support it. This will turn into an error in pytest 9. See: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/deprecations.html#sync-test-depending-on-async-fixture"

testcase.error:
self = <Coroutine test_200>
def setup(self) -> None: runner_fixture_id = f"_{self._loop_scope}_scoped_runner" if runner_fixture_id not in self.fixturenames: self.fixturenames.append(runner_fixture_id) > return super().setup() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/pytest_asyncio/plugin.py:458: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

@pytest.hookimpl(wrapper=True) def pytest_fixture_setup(fixturedef: FixtureDef, request) -> object | None: asyncio_mode = _get_asyncio_mode(request.config) if not _is_asyncio_fixture_function(fixturedef.func): if asyncio_mode == Mode.STRICT: # Ignore async fixtures without explicit asyncio mark in strict mode # This applies to pytest_trio fixtures, for example > return (yield) ^^^^^ E pytest.PytestRemovedIn9Warning: 'test_200' requested an async fixture 'client', with no plugin or hook that handled it. This is usually an error, as pytest does not natively support it. This will turn into an error in pytest 9. E See: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/deprecations.html#sync-test-depending-on-async-fixture
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/pytest_asyncio/plugin.py:728: PytestRemovedIn9Warning

testcase.name: test_404_group_unhandled_paths_on
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_middleware.TestMiddleware object at 0x1060fec30> testapp = <function testapp.<locals>._testapp at 0x106326fb0>
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_404_group_unhandled_paths_on(self, testapp): """test that an unknown path is captured in metrics if group_unhandled_paths=True"""
client = TestClient(testapp(group_unhandled_paths=True, filter_unhandled_paths=False)) > await client.get("/404") E TypeError: 'Response' object can't be awaited
tests/test_middleware.py:180: TypeError

self = <tests.test_middleware.TestMiddleware object at 0x1061c0050> testapp = <function testapp.<locals>._testapp at 0x10622ba00>

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_middleware.TestMiddleware object at 0x10619d450> testapp = <function testapp.<locals>._testapp at 0x106228f60>
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_histogram_custom_buckets(self, testapp): """test that custom histogram buckets appear after making requests"""
buckets = (10, 20, 30, 40, 50) client = TestClient(testapp(buckets=buckets)) > await client.get("/200") E TypeError: 'Response' object can't be awaited
tests/test_middleware.py:269: TypeError

@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_multi_prefix(self, testapp): """test that two collecting apps don't clash""" client1 = TestClient(testapp(prefix="app1")) client2 = TestClient(testapp(prefix="app2"))
> await client1.get("/200") E TypeError: 'Response' object can't be awaited
tests/test_middleware.py:391: TypeError

@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_custom_root_path(self, testapp): """test that custom root_path does not affect the path grouping"""
client = TestClient(testapp(skip_paths=["/health"]), root_path="/api")

testcase.name: test_200_with_always_use_int_status_set
test_200_always_use_int_status_set
test_str_default_labels
test_callable_default_values
test_async_callable
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_middleware.TestDefaultLabels object at 0x106126ea0> testapp = <function testapp.<locals>._testapp at 0x106431e80>
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_async_callable(self, testapp): """test that we can use an async callable to populate label values"""
async def async_bar(request): return "bar"
labels = { "bar": async_bar, "hello": "world", } client = TestClient(testapp(labels=labels)) > await client.get("/200") E TypeError: 'Response' object can't be awaited
tests/test_middleware.py:823: TypeError

testcase.name: test_from_header
test_from_header_allowed_values
test_from_header_allowed_values_disallowed_value
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_middleware.TestDefaultLabels object at 0x1061c05a0> testapp = <function testapp.<locals>._testapp at 0x1064d2fb0>
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_from_header_allowed_values_disallowed_value(self, testapp): """test with the library-provided from_header function"""
labels = { "foo": from_header("foo", allowed_values=("bar", "baz")), "hello": "world", } client = TestClient(testapp(labels=labels)) > await client.get("/200", headers={"foo": "zounds"}) E TypeError: 'Response' object can't be awaited
tests/test_middleware.py:872: TypeError

testcase.name: test_from_response_header
test_from_response_header_case_insensitive
test_from_response_header_http_exception
test_from_response_header_unhandled_exception
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_middleware.TestDefaultLabels object at 0x10621a4e0> testapp = <function testapp.<locals>._testapp at 0x1063254e0>
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_from_response_header_unhandled_exception(self, testapp): """test from_response_header function against an endpoint that raises an unhandled exception""" labels = {"foo": from_response_header("foo"), "hello": "world"} client = TestClient(testapp(labels=labels))
# make the test call. This raises an error but will still populate metrics. with pytest.raises(KeyError, match="value_error"): await client.get("/unhandled")
> metrics = await client.get("/metrics") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: 'Response' object can't be awaited
tests/test_middleware.py:938: TypeError

testcase.name: test_from_response_header_default
test_from_response_header_allowed_values
test_from_response_header_allowed_values_disallowed
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_middleware.TestDefaultLabels object at 0x1062402f0> testapp = <function testapp.<locals>._testapp at 0x106131900>
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_from_response_header_allowed_values_disallowed(self, testapp): """test with the library-provided from_response_header function, with a header value that does not match any of the allowed_values"""
labels = { "foo": from_response_header("foo", allowed_values=("bar", "bam")), "hello": "world", } client = TestClient(testapp(labels=labels)) > await client.get("/200", headers={"foo": "zounds"}) E TypeError: 'Response' object can't be awaited
tests/test_middleware.py:992: TypeError

testcase.name: test_exemplar
test_exemplar_request[False]

failure.message: Failed: async def functions are not natively supported. You need to install a suitable plugin for your async framework, for example: - anyio - pytest-asyncio - pytest-tornasync - pytest-trio - pytest-twisted

testcase.failure: async def functions are not natively supported. You need to install a suitable plugin for your async framework, for example: - anyio - pytest-asyncio - pytest-tornasync - pytest-trio - pytest-twisted
